text:
User: it's impossible to estimate what a healthy project's wiki in how many guides it has? that's nonsense! the prompt shouldn't say that at all.

yes, a) route should output a topic -- and route should have in its context a full view of everything that currently exists and what each entry is about (i.e. title and summary). [...] **#1 — the count nonsense: FIXED and committed (`21617b2`).** You were right; I deleted *"a healthy whole-project wiki is ~25-30 guides"* entirely. The altitude rule is now your model — "one coherent topic a reader opens under one title; split only at real topic seams" — with an explicit *don't target any guide count*. I also ripped out the project-specific "CONCRETE MERGES" examples (they were pc's own inject/embeddings/daemon noise baked into a general prompt). This is a real capture-path change: future captures use the new altitude.
 [...] User: it's impossible to estimate what a healthy project's wiki in how many guides it has? that's nonsense! the prompt shouldn't say that at all.
